questions.questionText: What are the leading causes of maternal mortality in the US?
options: mental health conditions, hemorrhage, cardiac conditions
questions.correctAnswer: mental health conditions, hemorrhage, cardiac conditions

questions.questionText: What parameters define infant mortality?
Which of the following places the patient at higher risk of postpartum hemorrhage?
Postpartum client saturates peri-pad within 15 minutes.  What is the priority nurse intervention?
This tool is used prior to discharge to assess their emotional state
Most common postpartum infection seen with cesarean delivery, prolonged labor, or prolonged ROM
questions.explanationText: Endometritis is an infection of the endometrium, the inner lining of the uterus. It most commonly occurs after childbirth, especially following a cesarean section.  Chorioamnionitis is an infection of the amniotic fluid, placenta, and fetal membranes (chorion and amnion) during pregnancy.  Group B Streptococcus (GBS) is a type of bacteria commonly found in the vagina and rectum of healthy adults.

questions.questionText: What are the four T's of postpartum hemorrhage
Decrease in fetal heart rate caused by cord compression
questions.explanationText: Variable decelerations = umbilical cord compression + appear as 'V' shape on monitor. Early decelerations = fetal head compression (benign). Late decelerations = uteroplacental insufficiency. Prolonged decelerations = various causes (prolonged cord compression, maternal hypotension, uterine hyperstimulation).

questions.questionText: The built environment can be assessed through which of the following
capacity to obtain, interpret, and use basic health info and services
learning theory based on observation of others
questions.explanationText: Cognitive learning theory: Learning occurs through mental processes like thinking, memory, and problem-solving, where learners actively organize and store information. Humanistic learning theory: Learning is driven by personal growth and self-actualization, emphasizing emotions, motivation, and the learner’s individual needs. Social learning theory: People learn by observing others, imitating behaviors, and modeling actions they see reinforced in their environment. Experiential learning theory: Learning happens through direct experience, reflection, and applying what is learned to real-world situations.
